reasons: Angela can attach to Masha during a side-lane dive or chase, adding protection without needing to rotate physically.
Masha's isolated pressure becomes stronger when Angela can support the commit from distance.

proof.summary: Masha wants to force isolated fights, and Angela can turn that isolated fight into a stronger commit.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches as Masha begins the chase.
Masha has an exit path after forcing the fight.
failureCases: Masha dives into multiple enemies without an exit path.
Angela attaches after Masha has already lost too much HP.
The enemy kites Masha until Angela's support window ends.

reasons: Floryn's global heal fits Masha's side-lane pressure because Masha often fights far from the main team.
Masha can continue split pressure while still receiving support during dangerous trades.

proof.summary: Masha can pressure side lanes while Floryn still supports her from across the map.
worksBestWhen: Floryn watches Masha's side-lane all-in timing.
Masha uses the heal to survive and disengage after forcing pressure.
failureCases: Masha is chain-controlled before healing matters.
Masha continues diving after the heal instead of resetting.

reasons: Chip can convert Masha's side-lane pressure into a fast team rotation or objective collapse.
Masha can pull enemies to a side lane while Chip helps teammates punish the response.

proof.summary: Masha pulls enemies to side lanes, and Chip can help teammates move quickly to punish that response.
worksBestWhen: Chip portals the team after enemies commit to stopping Masha.
The team has vision around the portal destination.
failureCases: The team uses the portal without vision.
Chip rotates before Masha has created pressure.
Masha dies before the team can collapse.

reasons: Mathilda can give Masha another reposition option when Masha dives or exits after forcing pressure.
Masha's chase and disengage patterns become more flexible with an extra movement path.

proof.summary: Masha wants to chase and disengage on her terms, and Mathilda adds an extra movement path.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda stays near the fight edge rather than diving too deep.
Masha uses the movement option after forcing enemy cooldowns.
failureCases: Masha ignores the exit route and overcommits.
Mathilda is too far away to provide the movement option.
The enemy saves crowd control for Masha's escape.

reasons: Rafaela's speed and heal support Masha's chase pattern and help her rotate between side pressure and team fights.
Masha can use the movement support to pressure targets and reposition after forcing responses.

proof.summary: Masha pressures through movement and repeated engages, while Rafaela improves speed and sustain.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela rotates with Masha before objective fights.
Masha uses the speed to engage or reset rather than chase too far.
failureCases: Masha plays fully isolated and receives no nearby support.
Rafaela is caught while following Masha.
The enemy uses hard control to stop Masha's chase.
